candidate_plans.name: Broad surveillance coverage
actions: spread available resources across all scheduled targets
maintain broad-area tracking continuity
defer prioritization changes until updated risk evidence arrives
expected_effects: reduces chance of losing lower-priority tracks
assumptions: coverage is preferred over concentrated monitoring

candidate_plans.name: Priority monitoring and reassessment
actions: focus available resources on highest-priority tracks
increase observation cadence for selected targets
reassess threat ranking after the next observation window
expected_effects: improves confidence on the highest-risk items
keeps the plan in decision-support mode
assumptions: highest-risk tracks should receive first attention

candidate_plans.name: Resource-sparing watch
actions: monitor only the top-priority target with minimum viable resources
hold remaining resources for follow-up tasking
escalate to broader coverage if risk increases
expected_effects: accepts reduced coverage for lower-priority targets
assumptions: resource conservation is valuable in the current window

evidence.title: Broad coverage preference
evidence.text: When broad coverage is requested, plans that cover all scheduled targets should receive favorable explanation, even if they consume more resources.

evidence.title: Pending authorization
evidence.text: Pending authorization is not a blocking failure by itself, but it requires human review before the plan can be marked approved for demo handoff.

evidence.title: Restricted boundary caution
evidence.text: Plans that mention restricted boundaries or restricted zones require review. If a plan combines restricted-zone language with direct execution wording, it must be blocked for this demo.

evidence.title: Human review for high-risk recommendations
evidence.text: Plans involving high-risk targets, blocked constraints, or uncertain authorization must require human review before any downstream handoff.

evidence.title: Simulation-only boundary
evidence.text: All generated plans in this demo remain simulation-only decision-support artifacts. A plan may be handed to the next demo module only as structured analysis, not as an execution command.

evidence.title: Approved authorization
evidence.text: An approved authorization state may support demo handoff only when the selected plan is within the authorization scope and has not expired.
